title: "Reader" App with Offline Support
description:
OBJECTIVE

Create an iOS Reader app that fetches and displays news articles using a public API (e.g. NewsAPI.org or JSONPlaceholder). The app should support offline viewing, dynamic UI, and clean architecture.

FEATURES TO IMPLEMENT

1. Fetch Articles
- Fetch a list of articles using a REST API (URLSession or any library).
- Display title, author, and thumbnail image (if available).

2. Offline Caching
- Store fetched articles locally (e.g. Core Data, Realm, or file caching).
- When offline, show cached data.

3. Pull-to-Refresh
- Refresh article list via UIRefreshControl.

4. Search Articles
- Add a search bar to filter news titles.

5. Bookmark Articles (Bonus)
- Allow the user to bookmark articles.
- Provide a "Bookmarks" tab to view saved articles.

EXPECTATIONS

Architecture:
- Use MVVM, Clean Architecture, or any modern pattern.
- Maintain proper separation of concerns.

Testing (Bonus):
- Add basic unit tests (optional).

UI:
- Use UIKit.
- Adaptive layout - Auto Layout.
- Light/Dark mode support.

Libraries (Optional):
- SDWebImage, Kingfisher, Alamofire, etc. (optional, but explain usage in the README).

Submission Instructions:
- Push code to a public repo (GitHub/Bitbucket).

title: User Focus & Productivity App
description:
OBJECTIVE

A user can enter one of four predefined Focus Modes (Work, Play, Rest, Sleep). Once they're in a particular mode, that focus mode starts a timer.

TIMER BEHAVIOR

- The timer shows the current elapsed time in 00:00 (mm:ss) form; once it crosses an hour mark, switch to 00:00:00 (hh:mm:ss) format.
- Every 2 minutes, the user earns a Point and a badge.

BADGES

Assign any ONE badge at random (from any of the three types below) for every point earned:
1. Trees: a leafy/tree-themed set of emoji badges.
2. Leaves & Fungi: a leaf/mushroom-themed set of emoji badges.
3. Animals: an animal-themed set of emoji badges.

SESSION LIFECYCLE

- The focus mode stays active until the user manually taps the "Stop Focusing" button.
- Handle the edge case where the user closes the app mid-session:
  - Ideally, the user should be redirected back into the SAME focus mode that was active.
  - Session points earned so far shouldn't be lost.

NAVIGATION & PROFILE

- From the Home View, add a button to navigate to the Profile View.
- Prefill a user name and a user image, and persist them to the DB.

PROFILE VIEW LAYOUT

- User image, centered.
- User name.
- Total points accumulated.
- Total badges earned (show the badges themselves).
- Recent Sessions - a list of recent sessions.

Each session in that list should show:
- Name
- Duration of the session
- Points earned
- Session start time

title: Notes App (iOS)
description:
OBJECTIVE

Build a Notes app for iOS that lets users create, edit, organize, and search personal notes, with reliable local persistence — the kind of self-contained CRUD-plus-search app that shows up constantly as a take-home assignment because it touches almost every fundamental iOS skill in one small surface area.

FEATURES TO IMPLEMENT

1. Create / Edit / Delete Notes
- A note has at minimum a title and a body, plus created and last-modified timestamps.
- Editing an existing note updates its last-modified timestamp.

2. List View
- Show all notes sorted by most-recently-modified first.
- Each row shows the title and a short preview snippet of the body.
- Support swipe-to-delete (or an equivalent explicit delete action).

3. Search
- A search field that filters the note list live as the user types, matching against both title and body.

4. Local Persistence
- Use Core Data (or an equivalent local store) so notes survive app relaunch.
- Handle the empty-state case (no notes yet) explicitly, not just a blank list.

5. Folders or Tags (Bonus)
- Let the user organize notes into folders, or attach tags, and filter the list by folder/tag.

6. Basic Rich Text or Markdown (Bonus)
- Support at least bold/italic formatting, or render Markdown syntax typed into the body.

7. iCloud Sync (Bonus)
- Sync notes across a user's devices via CloudKit (NSPersistentCloudKitContainer).

EXPECTATIONS

Architecture:
- Use MVVM or Clean Architecture; the ViewModel/business logic should be unit-testable independent of any UI framework.

UI:
- SwiftUI preferred (UIKit acceptable). Support Dark Mode and Dynamic Type.

Data layer:
- A real Core Data model (not an in-memory array standing in for persistence) with a plan for schema migration if the model changes later.

Performance:
- The list should stay smooth with several hundred notes; search filtering shouldn't visibly lag as the user types.

Testing (Bonus):
- Unit tests for the persistence layer and for the search-filtering logic.

Submission Instructions:
- Push code to a public repo, with a README explaining the architecture and any trade-offs made.

title: Image Library App (iOS)
description:
OBJECTIVE

Build an iOS app that browses a large library of images from a public API (e.g. Unsplash, Pexels, or any paginated image API), with smooth infinite-scroll browsing, real image caching, and a detail view — the classic "can you build a fast, memory-safe image grid" assignment.

FEATURES TO IMPLEMENT

1. Grid Browsing
- Fetch and display images in a scrollable grid (2-3 columns).

2. Pagination
- Load the next page of images automatically as the user scrolls near the bottom (infinite scroll), without re-fetching pages already loaded.

3. Image Caching
- Cache downloaded images (in memory and on disk) so scrolling back up, or relaunching the app, doesn't re-download images already fetched.

4. Detail View
- Tapping an image opens a full-screen detail view showing the image plus metadata (e.g. photographer/author, dimensions, description if the API provides one).

5. Favorites (Bonus)
- Let the user favorite images, persisted locally, with a dedicated "Favorites" tab.

6. Search (Bonus)
- Search the image library by keyword.

7. Download / Share (Bonus)
- Save an image to the photo library, or share it via the system share sheet.

EXPECTATIONS

Architecture:
- Use MVVM or Clean Architecture, with clearly separated networking, caching, and presentation layers.

UI:
- SwiftUI (LazyVGrid) or UIKit (UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout) — either way, the grid must be lazy so off-screen cells aren't created/laid out eagerly, and scrolling should stay smooth even as hundreds of images load in.

Networking:
- URLSession or a library, with explicit loading/error/empty states — not just a silently blank screen on failure.

Caching:
- A real in-memory + disk cache (NSCache plus a file-based disk layer, or a library like Kingfisher/SDWebImage) that avoids re-downloading and decodes images off the main thread.

Memory:
- Downsample large images to their actual on-screen display size rather than decoding them at full resolution — a genuine, measurable concern for a scrolling grid holding many images at once.

Testing (Bonus):
- Unit tests for the pagination logic and for cache hit/miss behavior.

Submission Instructions:
- Push code to a public repo, with a README explaining the caching/pagination approach.
